content.thinking:
I have all the information I need. The bug is clear and straightforward. Let me now write the plan file and then exit plan mode.

The bug is in `src/utils.ts` in the `processItems` function:
- Current: `for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++)` — stops one before the last element
- Fix: `for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++)` — processes all elements

The tests already cover this case and will pass once the fix is applied.

input.content:
# Fix: Off-by-One Bug in `processItems`

## Context

Users report the last item in the array is never processed by `processItems` in `src/utils.ts`. The root cause is a classic off-by-one error in the `for` loop condition: `i < items.length - 1` causes the loop to stop one iteration early, skipping the final element. The fix is a single-character change.

## Critical Files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/utils.ts` | Contains the buggy `processItems` function |
| `tests/utils.test.ts` | Existing tests — several already fail due to the bug |

## Bug Details

**File**: `src/utils.ts`, `processItems` function

```typescript
// BUGGY — stops before the last element
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {

// FIXED — processes all elements
for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
```

The subtraction of `1` from `items.length` means:
- A 3-item array only iterates indices 0 and 1 (skips index 2)
- A 1-item array iterates 0 times (returns empty array instead of 1 item)
- An empty array still returns `[]` correctly (no regression)

## Implementation Steps

1. **Edit `src/utils.ts`**: Change the loop condition from `i < items.length - 1` to `i < items.length`.

## Failing Tests (will pass after fix)

- `processItems > should process all items in the array` — expects `["HELLO", "WORLD", "FOO"]`, currently gets `["HELLO", "WORLD"]`
- `processItems > should handle a single item` — expects `["TEST"]`, currently gets `[]`
- `processItems > should handle two items` — expects `["A", "B"]`, currently gets `["A"]`

The `should return empty array for empty input` test already passes and will continue to pass.

## Verification

Run the test suite with:
```bash
npm test
```

All 6 tests (4 for `processItems`, 2 for `sum`) should pass.

content.text:
## Plan Summary

**Root cause identified:** Classic off-by-one error in `src/utils.ts`.

### The Bug

In `processItems`, the loop condition is `i < items.length - 1`, which causes it to stop *one iteration early*, skipping the last element:

```typescript
// BUGGY
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {

// FIXED — remove the "- 1"
for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
```

### Impact on existing tests

3 of the 4 `processItems` tests are currently **failing**:
| Test | Expected | Actual (buggy) |
|------|----------|----------------|
| 3-item array | `["HELLO","WORLD","FOO"]` | `["HELLO","WORLD"]` |
| 1-item array | `["TEST"]` | `[]` |
| 2-item array | `["A","B"]` | `["A"]` |

The empty-array test already passes and will not regress.

### Fix

**One change:** `src/utils.ts` — remove `- 1` from the loop condition. All 6 tests will pass after.

content.text:
All 6 tests pass. Here's a summary of what was changed:

**`src/utils.ts` — one-character fix:**

```diff
- for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {
+ for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
```

The `- 1` in the loop condition was the culprit — it caused the loop to stop one iteration before the last element. Removing it makes the loop iterate over every index from `0` to `items.length - 1` (inclusive), processing all items. I also removed the stale "Known issue" comment from the JSDoc.


The `- 1` in the loop condition was the culprit — it caused the loop to stop one iteration before the last element. Removing it makes the loop iterate over every index from `0` to `items.length - 1` (inclusive), processing all items. I also removed the stale "Known issue" comment from the JSDoc.
